segments.text: Later that day Jesus left the house, and sat down to teach beside the sea.
So many people gathered around him that he got into a boat and sat down there to teach, while all the crowds stood on the beach.
He explained many things to them, using stories as illustrations. {
The sower went out to sow,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he began.
As he was sowing, some of the seeds fell on the path. The birds came and ate them up.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Other seeds fell on stony ground where there wasn't much earth where they sprouted quickly.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The sun rose and scorched them and they withered because they had no roots.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Other seeds fell among thorns that grew up and choked them.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Still other seeds fell on good soil. They produced a harvest—some one hundred, some sixty, and some thirty times what had been planted.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Anybody who has ears should be listening!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The disciples came to Jesus and asked him, {
Why do you use illustrations when you speak to the people?
segments.speaker: The disciples came to Jesus and

segments.text: You're privileged to have revealed to you the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven, but they're not given such insights,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Those who already have will have more given to them, more than enough. But those who don't have, whatever they have will be taken away from them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That's why I speak to them in illustrations. For even though they see, they don't see; and even though they hear, they don't hear; nor do they understand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled in them: ‘Even though you hear, you won't understand, and even though you see, you won't perceive.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They have a hard-hearted attitude, they don't want to listen, and they've closed their eyes. If they didn't they might be able to see with their eyes, hear with their ears, and understand in their minds. Then they could return to me and I would heal them.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Your eyes are blessed, for they see. Your ears are blessed too, for they hear.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I'm telling you, many prophets and good people longed to see what you're seeing, but didn't see it. They longed to hear what you're hearing, but didn't hear it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So listen to the story of the sower.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When people hear the message about the kingdom and don't understand it, the evil one comes along and rips out what was sown in them. This is what happens to the seeds sown on the path.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The seeds sown on stony ground are people who hear the message and happily accept it straight away.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They last for a while, but because they don't have roots, when problems and troubles come, they quickly fall away.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The seeds sown among thorns are people who hear the message, but then life's worries and the temptation of money choke the message so that they become fruitless.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The seeds sown on good soil are people who hear the message, and understand it, and who produce a good harvest—some one hundred, some sixty, and some thirty times what was sown.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he told them another illustrated story: {
The kingdom of heaven is like a farmer who sowed good seeds in his field.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But while his workers were sleeping, an enemy came and sowed weed seeds on top of the wheat. Then they left.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So when the wheat grew and produced ears of grain, the weeds also grew up.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The farmer's workers came and asked him, ‘Sir, didn't you sow good seeds in your field? Where did the weeds come from?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Some enemy has done this,’ he replied. ‘Do you want us to go and pull up the weeds?’ they asked him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘No,’ he answered, ‘as you pull up the weeds, you might uproot the wheat too.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Let them both grow until harvest, and then at harvest-time I'll tell the reapers to first gather the weeds, tie them up into bundles and burn them, and then gather the wheat and store it in my barn.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He gave them another illustration: {
The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a farmer sowed in his field.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Even though it's the tiniest of seeds it grows much bigger than other plants. In fact it grows into a tree big enough for birds to roost in its branches.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He told them another illustrated story: {
The kingdom of heaven is like yeast that a woman mixed with a large quantity of flour, until all the dough was raised.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus explained all these things to the crowds using illustrated stories—in fact he didn't speak to them without using stories.
This fulfilled the prophet's words: {
I will speak using stories, and I will explain things hidden from the creation of the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus left the crowds and went into a house. His disciples came over to him, and asked him, {
Please explain to us the story about the weeds in the field.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The one who sows the good seed is the Son of man,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus explained.
The field is the world. The good seeds are the children of the kingdom. The weed seeds are the children of the evil one.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The enemy that sowed the weed seeds is the devil. The harvest is the end of the world. The reapers are angels.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Just as the weeds are harvested and burned so it will be at the end of the world.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The Son of man will send out his angels, and they will gather up every sinful thing and everyone who does evil,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and will throw them into the blazing furnace, where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then those who live right will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father.  Anybody with ears should be listening!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. A man found it, reburied it, and then full of joy went and sold all he had and bought that field.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The kingdom of heaven is also like a trader looking for good pearls.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he found the most expensive pearl ever he went and sold all he had and bought it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or again, the kingdom of heaven is like a fishing net thrown into the sea that caught all kinds of fish.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When it was full it was dragged ashore. The good fish were put into baskets while the bad ones were thrown away.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That's the way it will be when the end of the world comes. The angels will go out and separate the evil people from the good,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and throw them into the blazing furnace where there will be weeping and grinding of teeth.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Now do you understand everything?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yes,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied.
This is why every religious teacher who's learned about the kingdom of heaven is like a house-owner who brings out from his storeroom both new and old treasures,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus said.
After Jesus finished telling these stories, he left.
He went back to his home town and taught in the synagogue there. People were amazed, and asked, {
Where does he get his wisdom and miracles from?
segments.speaker: People were amazed, and

segments.text: Isn't this the carpenter's son? Isn't his mother's name Mary, and his brothers James, and Joseph, and Simon, and Judas?
segments.speaker: People were amazed, and

segments.text: Don't his sisters live here among us? So where does he get all this from?
segments.speaker: People were amazed, and

segments.text: And so they refused to believe in him. {
A prophet is honored everywhere except in his homeland and in his family,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told them.
Since they failed to trust in him, he did not do many miracles there.
